message:
test: adapt test suite to fault containment semantics and add generational/validation coverage

This commit updates the entire test suite to be policy-aware with respect
to the active fault containment mode, and adds new tests covering the
generational handle safety and input validation changes from the
preceding commits.

New tests for generational handle safety:
- test_cleanup.c: cleanup_stale_handle_rejected_after_drain_reuse
  verifies that a handle obtained before drain+reuse is rejected with
  ASX_E_NOT_FOUND after the slot is reused with a new generation.
- test_cleanup.c: cleanup_lifo_pop_reuse_does_not_false_exhaust
  verifies that repeated push/pop cycles on the same slot do not
  falsely exhaust capacity, validating the count-shrink logic.
- test_timer_wheel.c: timer_stale_handle_rejected_after_reset verifies
  that a handle from a pre-reset epoch is rejected after wheel reset,
  while a fresh handle to the same slot works correctly.

New tests for input validation:
- test_mpsc.c: create_rejects_non_region_handle verifies that passing
  a TASK-type handle to asx_channel_create returns ASX_E_INVALID_ARGUMENT.
- test_mpsc.c: create_rejects_closed_region verifies that channel
  creation against a drained region returns ASX_E_INVALID_STATE.
- test_trace.c: replay_reference_rejects_over_capacity verifies that
  asx_replay_load_reference rejects arrays exceeding ASX_TRACE_CAPACITY.

New tests for parallel scheduler parity:
- test_parallel.c: parallel_run_captured_state_dtor_on_complete verifies
  that captured-state destructors fire exactly once on task completion
  in the parallel scheduler path.
- test_parallel.c: parallel_single_worker_trace_matches_core_scheduler
  verifies that a single-worker parallel run produces the same trace
  event sequence as the core scheduler for identical deterministic
  workloads.

Fault-containment-aware test adaptations:
- All tests that previously assumed scheduler_run returns ASX_OK after
  a task error (the POISON_REGION behavior) now query
  asx_containment_policy_active() and branch assertions accordingly.
  Under non-poison policies (FAIL_FAST, ERROR_ONLY), the scheduler
  returns the fault status, and peer tasks may not have been polled yet.
- Affected tests: test_coroutine (co_error_produces_err_outcome),
  test_fault_containment (failing_task_does_not_block_other_tasks,
  scheduler_handles_fail_and_pending_mix), test_hft_microburst
  (hft_burst_errors_do_not_corrupt_peers), test_parallel
  (parallel_run_task_fails), test_scheduler
  (scheduler_task_failure_emits_complete), test_stress
  (fault_containment_multiple_failures_same_region),
  skeleton_test (failing_task_outcome_err).

E2E and vignette fixes:
- e2e_continuity_restart.c: Tighten scenario_crash_restart_simple to
  assert exact ASX_OK from continuity check instead of accepting
  ASX_E_REPLAY_MISMATCH.  Tighten scenario_corrupted_trace_detected
  to assert ASX_E_INVALID_ARGUMENT from import validation.
- vignette_obligations.c: Insert a scheduler_run + region_close
  sequence before drain in scenario_abort, because obligation
  reserve/abort is illegal once the region transitions OPEN -> CLOSING.
- vignette_replay.c: Add asx_trace_reset() before each scenario run
  to prevent stale trace state from prior scenarios.  Promote digest
  mismatch, continuity check failure, and import/replay mismatches
  from non-fatal "NOTE" observations to hard "FAIL" returns, since
  the underlying issues have been fixed and deterministic replay
  is now a correctness requirement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
